stories.title: CPSC Warning — Fasando Baby Lounger Linked to Infant Death; Manufacturer Refuses Recall

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: CPSC warning: stop using Fasando Baby Loungers immediately. An infant death was reported. ~6,200 sold on Amazon ~$35. Manufacturer has refused to recall. Remove foam padding, cut cover in half, dispose. Do not resell. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: The Fasando baby lounger is not recalled — the manufacturer refused. The CPSC can warn but cannot force a foreign company to act. Parents: if you have this lounger from Amazon (Aug 2024–Jul 2025, $35), stop using it now. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: CPSC Issues Urgent Warning on Fasando Baby Loungers — Infant Death Reported, Manufacturer Refuses Recall

research.facts:
CPSC Warning — Fasando Baby Lounger (Infant Death)
| Fact | Confidence | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| CPSC issued urgent warning (Notice of Violation, not formal recall) | HIGH | CPSC.gov/Warnings/2026 |
| Manufacturer: Fasando (seller: Qiyangshiqiguangyuekejiyouxiangongsi, China) | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
| ~6,200 units sold on Amazon, August 2024–July 2025, ~$35 | HIGH | CPSC.gov, Yahoo News |
| Hazard: sides too low; foot openings too wide (entrapment); no stand = fall hazard on elevated surface | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
| An infant death was reported (2-month-old placed in lounger on adult bed, found unresponsive) | HIGH | CPSC.gov, local12.com |
| Manufacturer refused recall / has not agreed to remedy | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
| Action: stop using, remove foam, cut cover in half, dispose, do not resell | HIGH | CPSC.gov |

stories.title: Two More Children's Products Recalled — Target Gigglescape Toy and GOPO Teething Toy

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: RECALL: Target's Gigglescape Under the Sea Popping Toy (~49,000 units, ~$10). Dome detaches, releasing small balls. One child choked. Return to any Target for a full refund. Sold Aug 2025–Jan 2026. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: RECALL: GOPO Toys Pull String Teething Toy (~70,410 units, Amazon $11–$15). Silicone strings can reach back of child's throat. Three choking incidents reported. Destroy toy and email for refund: recalls@gopotoys.com #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: Two Children's Product Recalls: Target Gigglescape Toy and GOPO Teething Toy — Both Choking Hazards

research.facts:
Target Gigglescape + GOPO Toys Recalls
**Target Gigglescape:**
| Fact | Confidence | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| Target Gigglescape Under the Sea Popping Toy recalled July 2, 2026 | HIGH | CPSC.gov/Recalls/2026 |
| ~49,000 units sold at Target, August 2025–January 2026, ~$10 | HIGH | CPSC.gov, KTLA |
| Hazard: clear plastic dome detaches; small balls inside = choking hazard | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
| 9 reports of dome detaching; 1 child began choking | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
| Remedy: return to any Target store for full refund, or request prepaid return label | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
**GOPO Toys:**
| Fact | Confidence | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| GOPO Toys Pull String Teething Toys recalled June 18, 2026 | HIGH | CPSC.gov/Recalls/2026 |
| ~70,410 units sold on Amazon, August 2023–March 2026, $11–$15 | HIGH | CPSC.gov, Fox Business |
| Disc-shaped teether, off-white with gray center ball, 6 multi-colored silicone pull strings | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
| Hazard: strings too small/long per mandatory standard; can reach back of throat = choking/respiratory distress risk | HIGH | CPSC.gov |
| 3 reports of choking or respiratory distress | HIGH | CPSC.gov, Daily Voice |
| Remedy: cut all strings, write "DESTROYED" on toy, email photo to recalls@gopotoys.com, full refund | HIGH | CPSC.gov |

stories.title: July 4 in Colorado Springs — What's Still Happening Today (Free and Family-Friendly)

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Colorado Springs July 4 guide: Rock Ledge Ranch 10 AM–4 PM ($7–$13, free ages 3 and under) | Banning Lewis Ranch FREE 3–10 PM (bounce houses 4–7 PM) | Switchbacks game 7 PM Weidner Field | Monument parade 10 AM #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: Nearly 8 in 10 Parents Say Summer Wrecks Their Child's Sleep — New Survey

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: 78% of parents say summer wrecks kids' sleep (KeaBabies, June 2026). Heat and longer daylight delay melatonin. Cool dark room, breathable pjs, consistent bedtime. This hits different in COS summer heat. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: 78% of Parents Say Summer Wrecks Their Child's Sleep — Here's Why and What Helps

research.facts:
KeaBabies Summer Sleep Survey
| Fact | Confidence | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| 78% of parents say summer negatively disrupts child's sleep routine | HIGH | PR Newswire / KeaBabies June 2026 survey |
| 55% of parents lose 1 or more hours of sleep per night due to child's summer sleep struggles | HIGH | PR Newswire |
| Survey: parents/guardians of children ages 0–4, conducted June 2026 | HIGH | PR Newswire |
| 57% adjusting family schedules; 50% actively searching for solutions | HIGH | PR Newswire |
| Root cause: higher ambient temperature disrupts body cooling; longer daylight delays melatonin | MEDIUM | Expert commentary cited in KeaBabies release |
| 77% of parents rank breathability as top priority for sleepwear | HIGH | PR Newswire |

stories.title: AAP Releases New Screen Time Framework — It's Less About Hours, More About What and How

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: The AAP updated screen time rules in 2026 — the 2-hour limit is out. New: 5 Cs framework (Child, Content, Calm, Crowding Out, Communication). Still firm: no screens under 18 months; device-free bedrooms and meals. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

article.headline: AAP Replaces the 2-Hour Screen Time Rule With a More Practical Framework — Here's What Changed

research.facts:
AAP New Screen Time "5 Cs" Framework
| Fact | Confidence | Source |
|------|-----------|--------|
| AAP released updated screen time guidance in early 2026 (February 2026) | HIGH | EdSurge Feb 5, 2026; CHOC Health blog |
| New framework: "5 Cs" — Child, Content, Calm, Crowding Out, Communication | HIGH | AAP.org, EdSurge, CHOC |
| No longer a simple 2-hour daily limit — approach is more nuanced | HIGH | EdSurge, CHOC |
| Still firm: No screens under 18 months except video chat | HIGH | AAP.org, HealthyChildren.org |
| Still firm: Ages 2–5, limit to ~1 hour high-quality content on weekdays | HIGH | AAP.org |
| New emphasis: device-free bedrooms and mealtimes; no screens 1 hour before bed | HIGH | AAP.org, CHOC |
| Recommend written "Family Media Plan" | MEDIUM | CHOC, EdSurge (citing AAP) |
